source: Case study booty building intake form.pdf
strategy: agentic chunking via Gemini (headline + summary + original text)

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

PERSONAL TRAINING CLIENT INTAKE FORM
Please fill in and save this form in Word, Google Docs or ODS.
The more information you provide, the better I will be able to assist you.
You can use either the imperial or metric system to fill in this form. Just
state which unit you used (e.g. cm, kg, lb, inch).

General
● Full legal name: Z
● Age: 20
● Height: 5'4
● Weight: 126-128lbs
● Body fat percentage (plus measurement technique): not sure
● Years of training experience: 1 year powerlifting/bodybuilding. 2+
years being active

Strength
For the section below, please give your current maximum abilities in
weight times reps, e.g. 250 lb x 6.
● Bench press: 120x5
headings: PERSONAL TRAINING CLIENT INTAKE FORM
General
Strength

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

● Squat (excluding body weight): not sure, stopped squatting a while
ago
● Chin-up (excluding body weight): weighted 12.5 lbs x9-10
● Overhead press: 60lbs x5
● Deadlifts ( since I don't squat ) : 245lbs x6
● hipthrusts : 245 x8 with bands

What are your fitness goals?
Aesthetics. I want to build MASSIVE glutes, maybe some hamstrings too,
but main focus is definitely the glutes. Im pretty happy with my muscular
development in other parts but wouldn't mind growing a little more overall
, but mostly want to maintain a feminine figure, bc I tend to put on width
easily.
Want to get and stay very lean. I know contest level leanness is not
sustainable, but I do want to maintain at a level where I can have good
muscle definition . I will attach an example of what I want to look like in
terms of muscular development and the level of leanness.

Do you have any muscle groups you do nót want to get bigger?
Quads. That's the main reason why I stopped squatting. I also have a pretty wide back so don't want
to put on anymore width. Also calves.
Will you be performing any other form of exercise alongside
strength training (e.g. soccer, yoga, Olympic Weightlifting)?
Not really, maybe some walking here and there.

On the following 7-point scale, how would you rate your
dedication level?
1: I want some results for the minimum amount of effort.
4: I want to strike a reasonable balance between good results in
proportion to effort and sustainability.
headings: What are your fitness goals?
Do you have any muscle groups you do nót want to get bigger?
Will you be performing any other form of exercise alongside
strength training (e.g. soccer, yoga, Olympic Weightlifting)?
On the following 7-point scale, how would you rate your
dedication level?
1: I want some results for the minimum amount of effort.
4: I want to strike a reasonable balance between good results in
proportion to effort and sustainability.

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

7: I will do whatever it takes to achieve maximum results.
7, will do whatever I can given my circumstances.

How often in a week would you be prepared to train for maximal
results?
6

What is your occupation?
Student. Interning at an accounting firm this summer, may be stressful
here and there. Also can only lift after work, which is 6PM or later.

Please list any pathologies and injuries (disabilities, allergies,
illnesses, syndromes, disorders etc.) you have or had:

Eating disorder tendencies. Tend to stress out , perfectionist mindset

Do you insist on any special diet (e.g. ketogenic, vegan)?
nope

Do you plan to use any PEDs while working together? If so, please
list them:
no

results?

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Lifestyle
Are there any times at which you are unable or unwilling to train?
This includes when you're at work. If you don't answer this
question acurately with detailed times, you may get a program you
can't follow(!):
have work on weekdays 8-6PM ish. Weekends free to train whenever

Activity level
Please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
● Sedentary (e.g. office job)

Stress level
Please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.

● Average stress (e.g. full-time work with deadlines and commuting)
headings: Lifestyle
Are there any times at which you are unable or unwilling to train?
This includes when you’re at work. If you don’t answer this
question acurately with detailed times, you may get a program you
can’t follow(!):
Activity level
Stress level

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

How is your sleep rhythm and quality?
6-7 hours a day, pretty good

How much caffeine do you consume daily on average or on a
typical work day?
one or two cups of coffee a day, preworkout sometimes

[Women only] Do you have a regular menstrual cycle? And are you
using any form of contraception? If these questions are sensitive,
feel free to ignore them, but the more information I have, the
better I can help you.
not regular. may have some hormonal issues. very long cycles

Equipment
Do you have skinfold calipers and if so, which one(s)?
nope

What are the lightest barbell weight plates you have access to?

2.5 lbs
What are the smallest weight increments between the sets of
dumbbells you have access to?
5 lbs

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Do you have access to…
● a squat cage or rack? Yes
● a 45° hyperextension bench? Yes
● a reverse hyper machine? no

● a glute-ham raise?no

● a standing calf raise machine? no
● a dip/chin-up belt? no
● a leg curl machine and if so, is it seated, lying or standing? both seated
and lying
● a pair of knee wraps? no
● gymnastic rings? no
● a TRX or similar suspension device? no
● powerlifting bands (not the light home workout stuff)? no
● powerlifting chains? no
● a seated calf raise and if so, is it closed chain where you push your
body up (see example image below) or different? no
headings: Do you have access to…

pages.text:
BAYESIANBODYBUILDING.COM

Does your available equipment differ from that in most gyms in
any other way (e.g. you train in a Crossfit box or at home)?
no

Supplements
Fish oil, borage oil, selenium, ashwanganda, phenibut ( rarely), protein
powder, PEScience high volume

Genetics
● Wrist circumference (smallest point): 14.5 cm
● Ankle circumference (smallest point): 20.5 cm
● How much did you weigh before you started lifting and what was
your height at that point? 115 lbs, 5'4
● Were you heavy as a baby? not sure
● Please attach a picture to this form in which the back of your right
hand is clearly visible. Ideally, place your right hand on a flat
surface with your fingers touching each other and then take a
picture of it.

Current Program
Please describe or attach your current diet plan in detail:
Attached program. Ignore the #s there, I stopped updating them. But
pretty much UL split repeated 3x a week. Been doing that for about 3
months. Recently went on a hiatus because of traveling and school, about
two weeks no lifting/tracking. Just started to get back into a rountine.

diet: prior to the hiatus, was hitting about 1950 calories, 220/133/60
headings: Does your available equipment differ from that in most gyms in
any other way (e.g. you train in a Crossfit box or at home)?

Genetics
